foundation.title: ERROR
foundation.subtitle: Exceptions
foundation.content: Exceptions are 'Special Events' that happen when a Java program crashes. You use 'Try/Catch' to catch the error before the program dies.
foundation.scenario: A Fire Alarm. If (the fire) occurs, the alarm (Exception) sounds. You catch (catch) the alarm and exit the building safely instead of burning.

examples.title: Try Block
examples.explanation: Defining a code area where a crash might occur safely.

examples.title: Catch Block
examples.explanation: The room you go to if a crash actually happens in the try.

examples.title: Finally
examples.explanation: A block that ALWAYS runs, whether you crashed or didn't.

foundation.keyPoint: try, catch, finally. The safety loop.

foundation.title: FILEIO
foundation.subtitle: Reading Disk
foundation.content: Java uses 'Streams' to read from files on your disk. You must always close these streams to avoid leaking memory.
foundation.scenario: A Paper Document in a file cabinet. You open the cabinet (FileReader), read the page (read), and close it (close) so others can use it.

examples.title: FileReader
examples.explanation: Creating a link to a file on your hard drive easily.

examples.title: Buffered Read
examples.explanation: Reading the file one whole line at a time to be faster.

examples.title: Close File
examples.explanation: Manually closing the stream to free up system resources.

foundation.keyPoint: Always wrap File I/O in a try-catch-finally.

foundation.title: PACKAGE
foundation.subtitle: Toolboxes
foundation.content: Packages are folders of related Java classes. You 'import' a package to get tools like the Scanner or the ArrayList.
foundation.scenario: A Toolbox. Each drawer (Package) has tools (Classes) for a different job. You open the 'Tools' drawer (import java.util.*) to get the Scanner.

examples.title: Standard Lib
examples.explanation: Importing a box of pre-made tools from the official Java library.

examples.title: Custom Pack
examples.explanation: Creating your own package folder at the top of a file.

examples.title: Specific One
examples.explanation: Importing just one specific tool instead of the whole drawer.

foundation.keyPoint: The first line in a Java file is usually the package name.
